subject: [PATCH RFC 00/15] mm/slab: introduce alloc_flags and slab_alloc_context

reviews.author: Vlastimil (SUSE)
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch series does not remove the __GFP_NO_OBJ_EXT flag used by memory allocation profiling, which is still present in slab/for-next and will be modified by a later patch (patch 15) but cannot be used as-is due to this issue. The reviewer suggests finding an alternative solution to avoid using this gfp flag bit.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for the heads up. I missed it because my series is based on
slab/for-next and that commit is in mm-unstable. My patch 15 actually
modifies the TODO comment that is meanwhile resolved by Hao Ge's patch.

Which means my patch 15/15 can't be used as-is, and at worst I will drop it.
But I'd encourage Hao Ge with Suren to find some way to avoid the gfp flag
usage too, because it's now quite a niche use case (preventing false
positive CONFIG_MEM_ALLOC_PROFILING_DEBUG warnings, IIUC?) to take a
valuable gfp flag bit, IMHO.

2026-06-09.patch_summary: This patch introduces alloc_flags and slab_alloc_context to improve memory allocation profiling in the Linux kernel. The change is related to a previous commit that replaced a fixed-size PFN array with a dynamic linked list, and it ensures that __GFP_NO_OBJ_EXT is properly handled during memory allocation. This update aims to enhance the accuracy of memory allocation profiling.
